16	import urllib.request
17	
18	import seafile
19	
20	
21	DAEMON_START_TIMEOUT = 60  # seconds to wait for socket after seaf-cli start
22	INIT_TIMEOUT = 30          # seconds to wait for .ini after seaf-cli init
23	
24	
25	class BadConfiguration(Exception):
26	    pass
27	
28	
29	def get_configuration(variable: str, *args) -> Any:
30	    """Helper function to get a configuration.
31	    see https://gitlab.com/-/snippets/1941025
32	    """
33	    if args:
34	        default = args[0]
35	
36	    try:
37	        file = os.environ[f"{variable}_FILE"]
38	    except KeyError:
39	        pass
40	    else:
41	        with open(file, "rt") as fo:
42	            return fo.read()
43	
44	    try:
45	        return os.environ[variable]
46	    except KeyError:
47	        pass
48	
49	    try:
50	        return default
51	    except UnboundLocalError:
52	        raise BadConfiguration(
53	            f"Environment variable {variable} was not found but is required."
54	        )
55	
56	
57	class Client:
58	
59	    def __init__(self) -> None:
60	        self.username: str = get_configuration("SEAF_USERNAME")
61	        self.url: str = get_configuration("SEAF_SERVER_URL")
62	        self.skip_ssl_cert: bool = bool(get_configuration("SEAF_SKIP_SSL_CERT", None))
63	        self.upload_limit: int = get_configuration("SEAF_UPLOAD_LIMIT", None)
64	        self.download_limit: int = get_configuration("SEAF_DOWNLOAD_LIMIT", None)
65	        self.mfa_secret: str = get_configuration("SEAF_2FA_SECRET", None)
66	
67	        self.password: str = get_configuration("SEAF_PASSWORD", None)
68	        self.token: str = get_configuration("SEAF_TOKEN", None)
69	        if not (self.password or self.token):
70	            raise BadConfiguration(
71	                "At least one of SEAF_PASSWORD or SEAF_TOKEN are required."
72	            )
73	
74	        self.ini = Path.home().joinpath(".ccnet", "seafile.ini")
75	        self.log = Path.home().joinpath(".ccnet", "logs", "seafile.log")
76	        self.seafile = Path("/seafile")
77	        self.socket = self.seafile.joinpath("seafile-data", "seafile.sock")
78	        self.target = Path("/library")
79	
80	        if self.socket.exists():
81	            self.rpc = seafile.RpcClient(str(self.socket))
82	
83	        self.binary = ["seaf-cli"]
84	        self._get_librairies()
85	
86	    def _get_librairies(self):
87	        self.libraries = {}
88	
89	        single_library_variables = ["SEAF_LIBRARY", "SEAF_LIBRARY_UUID", "SEAF_LIBRARY_PASSWORD"]
90	        if any(environ in single_library_variables for environ in os.environ):
91	            logger.info("Single library detected. Multiple libraries will be ignored.")
92	            library = {}
93	
94	            uuid = None
95	            if legacy := os.getenv("SEAF_LIBRARY_UUID", None):
96	                logger.warning("SEAF_LIBRARY_UUID is obsolete, please use SEAF_LIBRARY instead.")
97	                uuid = legacy
98	            if current := os.getenv("SEAF_LIBRARY", None):
99	                uuid = current
100	
101	            if uuid is None:
102	                raise Exception("Please provide an UUID with SEAF_LIBRARY for single library usage.")
103	            library["uuid"] = uuid
104	
105	            if password := os.getenv("SEAF_LIBRARY_PASSWORD", None):
106	                library["password"] = password
107	
108	            self.libraries["_"] = library
109	            return
110	
111	        for variable in sorted(os.environ):
112	            if variable.startswith("SEAF_LIBRARY"):
113	                name = variable.split("_")[2].lower()
114	
115	                if "_PASSWORD" in variable:
116	                    password = get_configuration(variable, None)
117	                    try:
118	                        if password:
119	                            self.libraries[name]["password"] = password
120	                    except KeyError:
121	                        logger.warning(f"Cannot set a password to unknown library {name}")
122	                else:
123	                    self.libraries[name] = {}
124	                    uuid = os.environ[variable]
125	                    self.libraries[name]["uuid"] = uuid
126	
127	    def _get_credential_args(self) -> list[str]:
128	        return [
129	            "-s", self.url,
130	            "-u", self.username,
131	            *(["-T", self.token] if self.token else ["-p", self.password])
132	        ]
133	
134	    def _wait_for_path(self, path: Path, label: str, timeout: int) -> None:
135	        deadline = time.monotonic() + timeout
136	        while not path.exists():
137	            if time.monotonic() > deadline:
138	                logger.error(f"Timed out after {timeout}s waiting for {label} — daemon may have failed to start")
139	                sys.exit(1)
140	            logger.debug(f"Waiting for {label}...")
141	            time.sleep(1)
142	
143	    def _daemon_pid(self) -> int | None:
144	        for pidfile in [
145	            self.seafile / "seafile-data" / "seafile.pid",
146	            Path.home() / ".ccnet" / "seafile.pid",
147	        ]:
148	            if pidfile.exists():
149	                try:
150	                    return int(pidfile.read_text().strip())
151	                except (ValueError, OSError):
152	                    pass
153	        # fallback: scan /proc for seaf-daemon
154	        for entry in Path("/proc").iterdir():
155	            if not entry.name.isdigit():
156	                continue
157	            try:
158	                cmdline = (entry / "cmdline").read_bytes().replace(b"\x00", b" ").decode()
159	                if "seaf-daemon" in cmdline:
160	                    return int(entry.name)
161	            except OSError:
162	                pass
163	        return None
164	
165	    def initialize(self):
166	        logger.info("Initializing `seaf-cli`.")
167	        if not self.ini.exists():
168	            logger.info("Seafile .ini file not found, running `seaf-cli init`")
169	            result = subprocess.run(self.binary + ["init", "-d", str(self.seafile)])
170	            if result.returncode != 0:
171	                logger.error(f"`seaf-cli init` failed (exit {result.returncode})")
172	                sys.exit(1)
173	            self._wait_for_path(self.ini, "seafile.ini", INIT_TIMEOUT)
174	
175	        # Remove stale PID/socket left by a previous container (same volume, new PID
176	        # namespace) — seaf-cli start checks the PID file and exits 1 if that PID
177	        # currently exists (even if it's an unrelated process in the new namespace).
178	        for stale in [self.seafile / "seafile-data" / "seafile.pid", self.socket]:
179	            stale.unlink(missing_ok=True)
180	
181	        logger.info("Starting `seaf-cli`.")
182	        result = subprocess.run(self.binary + ["start"])
183	        if result.returncode != 0:
184	            logger.error(f"`seaf-cli start` failed (exit {result.returncode})")
185	            sys.exit(1)
186	        self._wait_for_path(self.socket, "seafile socket", DAEMON_START_TIMEOUT)
187	
188	        self.rpc = seafile.RpcClient(str(self.socket))
189	
190	    def configure(self):
191	        command = self.binary + ["config"]
192	        if self.skip_ssl_cert:
193	            subprocess.run(command + ["-k", "disable_verify_certificate", "-v", str(self.skip_ssl_cert)], check=True)
194	        if self.download_limit:
195	            subprocess.run(command + ["-k", "download_limit", "-v", self.download_limit], check=True)
196	        if self.upload_limit:
197	            subprocess.run(command + ["-k", "upload_limit", "-v", self.upload_limit], check=True)
198	
199	    def synchronize(self):
200	        core = [*self.binary, "sync", *self._get_credential_args()]
201	        for name, configuration in self.libraries.items():
202	            uuid = configuration["uuid"]
203	
204	            repository = self.rpc.get_repo(uuid)
205	            if repository is not None:
206	                logger.info(f"Library {name} is already synced.")
207	                continue
208	
209	            command = core + ["-l", uuid]
210	
211	            if "password" in configuration:
212	                command += ["-e", configuration["password"]]
213	
214	            target = self.target if name == "_" else self.target.joinpath(name)
215	            target.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
216	            command += ["-d", str(target)]
217	
218	            if self.mfa_secret:
219	                result = subprocess.run(
220	                    ["oathtool", "--base32", "--totp", self.mfa_secret],
221	                    capture_output=True, text=True, check=True,
222	                )
223	                command += ["-a", result.stdout.strip()]
224	
225	            # Redact credentials before logging
226	            secrets = {s for s in (self.password, self.token) if s}
227	            safe = ["***" if a in secrets else a for a in command]
228	            logger.debug(f"Running: {' '.join(safe)}")
229	
230	            result = subprocess.run(command)
231	            if result.returncode != 0:
232	                logger.error(f"`seaf-cli sync` for library {name!r} failed (exit {result.returncode})")
233	
234	    def watch(self):
235	        pid = self._daemon_pid()
236	        if pid is None:
237	            logger.error("Cannot locate seaf-daemon PID — exiting to trigger container restart")
238	            sys.exit(1)
239	        logger.info(f"Monitoring seaf-daemon (PID {pid})")
240	        while True:
241	            try:
242	                os.kill(pid, 0)
243	            except ProcessLookupError:
244	                logger.error(f"seaf-daemon (PID {pid}) has exited — restarting container")
245	                sys.exit(1)
246	            time.sleep(10)
247	
248	    def _collect_status(self) -> dict:
249	        """Gather current sync state for reporting to the nas-settings panel."""
250	        pid = self._daemon_pid()
251	        daemon_alive = False
252	        if pid is not None:
253	            try:
254	                os.kill(pid, 0)
255	                daemon_alive = True
256	            except ProcessLookupError:
257	                pass
258	
259	        repos = []
260	        if hasattr(self, "rpc"):
261	            try:
262	                rpc_repos = self.rpc.get_repo_list(-1, -1)
263	                auto_sync = self.rpc.is_auto_sync_enabled()
264	                for repo in rpc_repos:
265	                    entry: dict = {
266	                        "name": repo.name,
267	                        "id": repo.id,
268	                        "state": None,
269	                        "rate_kb": None,
270	                        "progress_pct": None,
271	                        "error": None,
272	                    }
273	                    if not auto_sync or not repo.auto_sync:
274	                        entry["state"] = "auto sync disabled"
275	                    else:
276	                        task = self.rpc.get_repo_sync_task(repo.id)
277	                        if task is None:
278	                            entry["state"] = "waiting"
279	                        else:
280	                            entry["state"] = task.state
281	                            if task.state in ("uploading", "downloading"):
282	                                tx = self.rpc.find_transfer_task(repo.id)
283	                                if tx is not None:
284	                                    if tx.block_total > 0:
285	                                        entry["progress_pct"] = round(
286	                                            tx.block_done / tx.block_total * 100, 1
287	                                        )
288	                                    entry["rate_kb"] = round(tx.rate / 1024.0, 1)
289	                            elif task.state == "error":
290	                                entry["error"] = self.rpc.sync_error_id_to_str(task.error)
291	                    repos.append(entry)
292	            except Exception:
293	                pass
294	
295	        ingest: dict = {}
296	        ingest_path = Path("/tmp/ingest-status.json")
297	        if ingest_path.exists():
298	            try:
299	                ingest = json.loads(ingest_path.read_text())
300	            except Exception:
301	                pass
302	
303	        paused = False
304	        if hasattr(self, "rpc"):
305	            try:
306	                paused = not self.rpc.is_auto_sync_enabled()
307	            except Exception:
308	                pass
309	
310	        return {
311	            "container_id": os.environ.get("HOSTNAME", "unknown"),
312	            "library_uuid": os.environ.get("SEAF_LIBRARY", ""),
313	            "reported_at": datetime.datetime.utcnow().isoformat() + "Z",
314	            "daemon_pid": pid,
315	            "daemon_alive": daemon_alive,
316	            "paused": paused,
317	            "repos": repos,
318	            "staging_files": ingest.get("files"),
319	            "last_ingest_at": ingest.get("last_ingest_at"),
320	            "ingest_days": ingest.get("ingest_days"),
321	        }
322	
323	    def _start_status_reporter(self, settings_url: str, token: str | None) -> None:
324	        """Start a daemon thread that POSTs sync status to the server every 30 seconds."""
325	        status_url = settings_url.rstrip("/").rsplit("/api/settings", 1)[0] + "/api/status"
326	
327	        def _loop() -> None:
328	            while True:
329	                try:
330	                    payload = json.dumps(self._collect_status()).encode()
331	                    headers = {"Content-Type": "application/json"}
332	                    if token:
333	                        headers["X-Status-Token"] = token
334	                    req = urllib.request.Request(
335	                        status_url, data=payload, headers=headers, method="POST"
336	                    )
337	                    with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=10) as resp:
338	                        body = json.loads(resp.read().decode())
339	                        cmd = body.get("command")
340	                        if cmd == "pause":
341	                            subprocess.run(["seaf-cli", "pause"], timeout=10, capture_output=True)
342	                            logger.info("Paused sync on server command")
343	                        elif cmd == "resume":
344	                            subprocess.run(["seaf-cli", "resume"], timeout=10, capture_output=True)
345	                            logger.info("Resumed sync on server command")
346	                except Exception:
347	                    pass  # best-effort; never crash the container
348	                time.sleep(30)
349	
350	        threading.Thread(target=_loop, daemon=True, name="status-reporter").start()
351	        logger.info("Status reporter started → %s", status_url)
352	
353	    def healthcheck(self) -> int:
354	        tasks = self.rpc.get_clone_tasks()
355	        healthy = True
356	        for task in tasks:
357	            name = task.repo_name
358	            state = task.state
359	
360	            if state == 'done':
361	                continue
362	            elif state == "fetch":
363	                tx_task = self.rpc.find_transfer_task(task.repo_id)
364	                percentage = 0 if tx_task.block_done == 0 else tx_task.block_done / tx_task.block_total * 100
365	                rate = 0 if tx_task.rate == 0 else tx_task.rate / 1024.0
366	                print(f"{name:<50s}\t{state:<20s}\t{percentage:<.1f}%, {rate:<.1f}KB/s")
367	            elif state == "error":
368	                healthy = False
369	                error = self.rpc.sync_error_id_to_str(task.error)
370	                print(f"{name:<50s}\t{state:<20s}\t{error}")
371	            else:
372	                print(f"{name:<50s}\t{state:<20s}")
373	
374	        repos = self.rpc.get_repo_list(-1, -1)
375	        auto_sync_enabled = self.rpc.is_auto_sync_enabled()
376	        for repo in repos:
377	            name = repo.name
378	
379	            if not auto_sync_enabled or not repo.auto_sync:
380	                print(f"{name:<50s}\t{'auto sync disabled':<20s}")
381	                continue
382	
383	            task = self.rpc.get_repo_sync_task(repo.id)
384	            if task is None:
385	                print(f"{name:<50s}\t{'waiting for sync':<20s}")
386	                continue
387	
388	            state = task.state
389	            if state in ['uploading', 'downloading']:
390	                tx_task = self.rpc.find_transfer_task(repo.id)
391	                if tx_task.rt_state == "data":
392	                    state += " files"
393	                    percentage = 0 if tx_task.block_done == 0 else tx_task.block_done / tx_task.block_total * 100
394	                    rate = 0 if tx_task.rate == 0 else tx_task.rate / 1024.0
395	                    print(f"{name:<50s}\t{state:<20s}\t{percentage:<.1f}%, {rate:<.1f}KB/s")
396	                elif tx_task.rt_state == "fs":
397	                    state += " files list"
398	                    percentage = 0 if tx_task.fs_objects_done == 0 else tx_task.fs_objects_done / tx_task.fs_objects_total * 100
399	                    print(f"{name:<50s}\t{state:<20s}\t{percentage:<.1f}%")
400	            elif state == 'error':
401	                healthy = False
402	                error = self.rpc.sync_error_id_to_str(task.error)
403	                print(f"{name:<50s}\t{state:<20s}\t{error}")
404	            else:
405	                print(f"{name:<50s}\t{state:<20s}")
406	
407	        return 0 if healthy else 1
408	
409	
410	debug = get_configuration("DEBUG", False)
411	level = logging.INFO
412	fmt = "%(asctime)s - %(levelname)s - %(message)s"
413	if debug:
414	    level = logging.DEBUG
415	    fmt = "%(asctime)s - %(filename)s:%(lineno)d - %(levelname)s - %(message)s"
416	logging.basicConfig(format=fmt, level=level)
417	logger = logging.getLogger()
418	
419	
420	if __name__ == "__main__":
421	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(prog="", description="", epilog="")
422	    parser.add_argument("--healthcheck", action="store_true")
423	    args = parser.parse_args()
424	
425	    if args.healthcheck:
426	        logger.disabled = True
427	
428	    try:
429	        client = Client()
430	    except BadConfiguration as e:
431	        logger.error(f"Bad configuration: {e}")
432	        sys.exit(1)
433	
434	    if args.healthcheck:
435	        if not hasattr(client, "rpc"):
436	            print("seaf-daemon socket not found — daemon is not running")
437	            sys.exit(1)
438	        sys.exit(client.healthcheck())
439	
440	    def _handle_stop(signum, frame):
441	        logger.info("Received signal — stopping seaf-daemon cleanly")
442	        subprocess.run(["seaf-cli", "stop"], timeout=30)
443	        sys.exit(0)
444	
445	    signal.signal(signal.SIGTERM, _handle_stop)
446	    signal.signal(signal.SIGINT, _handle_stop)
447	
448	    client.initialize()
449	    client.configure()
450	    client.synchronize()
451	
452	    settings_url = get_configuration("SEAF_SETTINGS_URL", None)
453	    status_token = get_configuration("SEAF_STATUS_TOKEN", None)
454	    if settings_url:
455	        client._start_status_reporter(settings_url, status_token)
456	
457	    client.watch()","total_lines":457}

323	    def _start_status_reporter(self, settings_url: str, token: str | None) -> None:
324	        """Start a daemon thread that POSTs sync status to the server every 30 seconds."""
325	        status_url = settings_url.rstrip("/").rsplit("/api/settings", 1)[0] + "/api/status"
326	
327	        def _loop() -> None:
328	            while True:
329	                try:
330	                    payload = json.dumps(self._collect_status()).encode()
331	                    headers = {"Content-Type": "application/json"}
332	                    if token:
333	                        headers["X-Status-Token"] = token
334	                    req = urllib.request.Request(
335	                        status_url, data=payload, headers=headers, method="POST"
336	                    )
337	                    with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=10) as resp:
338	                        body = json.loads(resp.read().decode())
339	                        cmd = body.get("command")
340	                        if cmd == "pause":
341	                            subprocess.run(["seaf-cli", "pause"], timeout=10, capture_output=True)
342	                            logger.info("Paused sync on server command")
343	                        elif cmd == "resume":
344	                            subprocess.run(["seaf-cli", "resume"], timeout=10, capture_output=True)
345	                            logger.info("Resumed sync on server command")
346	                except Exception:
347	                    pass  # best-effort; never crash the container
348	                time.sleep(30)
349	
350	        threading.Thread(target=_loop, daemon=True, name="status-reporter").start()
351	        logger.info("Status reporter started → %s", status_url)

367	            elif state == "error":

371	            else:

373

263	                auto_sync = self.rpc.is_auto_sync_enabled()
264	                for repo in rpc_repos:
265	                    entry: dict = {
266	                        "name": repo.name,
267	                        "id": repo.id,
268	                        "state": None,
269	                        "rate_kb": None,
270	                        "progress_pct": None,
271	                        "error": None,
272	                    }
273	                    if not auto_sync or not repo.auto_sync:
274	                        entry["state"] = "auto sync disabled"
275	                    else:
276	                        task = self.rpc.get_repo_sync_task(repo.id)
277	                        if task is None:
278	                            entry["state"] = "waiting"
279	                        else:
280	                            entry["state"] = task.state
281	                            if task.state in ("uploading", "downloading"):
282	                                tx = self.rpc.find_transfer_task(repo.id)
283	                                if tx is not None:
284	                                    if tx.block_total > 0:
285	                                        entry["progress_pct"] = round(
286	                                            tx.block_done / tx.block_total * 100, 1
287	                                        )
288	                                    entry["rate_kb"] = round(tx.rate / 1024.0, 1)
289	                            elif task.state == "error":
290	                                entry["error"] = self.rpc.sync_error_id_to_str(task.error)
291	                    repos.append(entry)
292	            except Exception:
293	                pass
294	
295	        ingest: dict = {}
296	        ingest_path = Path("/tmp/ingest-status.json")
297	        if ingest_path.exists():
298	            try:
299	                ingest = json.loads(ingest_path.read_text())
300	            except Exception:
301	                pass
302	
303	        paused = False
304	        if hasattr(self, "rpc"):
305	            try:
306	                paused = not self.rpc.is_auto_sync_enabled()
307	            except Exception:
308	                pass
309	
310	        return {
311	            "container_id": os.environ.get("HOSTNAME", "unknown"),
312	            "library_uuid": os.environ.get("SEAF_LIBRARY", ""),
313	            "reported_at": datetime.datetime.utcnow().isoformat() + "Z",
314	            "daemon_pid": pid,
315	            "daemon_alive": daemon_alive,
316	            "paused": paused,
317	            "repos": repos,
318	            "staging_files": ingest.get("files"),
319	            "last_ingest_at": ingest.get("last_ingest_at"),
320	            "ingest_days": ingest.get("ingest_days"),
321	        }
322	
323	    def _start_status_reporter(self, settings_url: str, token: str | None) -> None:
324	        """Start a daemon thread that POSTs sync status to the server every 30 seconds."""
325	        status_url = settings_url.rstrip("/").rsplit("/api/settings", 1)[0] + "/api/status"
326	
327	        def _loop() -> None:
328	            while True:
329	                try:
330	                    payload = json.dumps(self._collect_status()).encode()
331	                    headers = {"Content-Type": "application/json"}
332	                    if token:
333	                        headers["X-Status-Token"] = token
334	                    req = urllib.request.Request(
335	                        status_url, data=payload, headers=headers, method="POST"
336	                    )
337	                    with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=10) as resp:
338	                        body = json.loads(resp.read().decode())
339	                        cmd = body.get("command")
340	                        if cmd == "pause":
341	                            subprocess.run(["seaf-cli", "pause"], timeout=10, capture_output=True)
342	                            logger.info("Paused sync on server command")
343	                        elif cmd == "resume":
344	                            subprocess.run(["seaf-cli", "resume"], timeout=10, capture_output=True)
345	                            logger.info("Resumed sync on server command")
346	                except Exception:
347	                    pass  # best-effort; never crash the container
348	                time.sleep(30)
349	
350	        threading.Thread(target=_loop, daemon=True, name="status-reporter").start()
351	        logger.info("Status reporter started → %s", status_url)
352	
353	    def healthcheck(self) -> int:
354	        tasks = self.rpc.get_clone_tasks()
355	        healthy = True
356	        for task in tasks:
357	            name = task.repo_name
358	            state = task.state
359	
360	            if state == 'done':
361	                continue
362	            elif state == "fetch":
363	                tx_task = self.rpc.find_transfer_task(task.repo_id)
364	                percentage = 0 if tx_task.block_done == 0 else tx_task.block_done / tx_task.block_total * 100
365	                rate = 0 if tx_task.rate == 0 else tx_task.rate / 1024.0
366	                print(f"{name:<50s}\t{state:<20s}\t{percentage:<.1f}%, {rate:<.1f}KB/s")
367	            elif state == "error":
368	                healthy = False
369	                error = self.rpc.sync_error_id_to_str(task.error)
370	                print(f"{name:<50s}\t{state:<20s}\t{error}")
371	            else:
372	                print(f"{name:<50s}\t{state:<20s}")
373	
374	        repos = self.rpc.get_repo_list(-1, -1)
375	        auto_sync_enabled = self.rpc.is_auto_sync_enabled()
376	        for repo in repos:
377	            name = repo.name
378	
379	            if not auto_sync_enabled or not repo.auto_sync:
380	                print(f"{name:<50s}\t{'auto sync disabled':<20s}")
381	                continue
382	
383	            task = self.rpc.get_repo_sync_task(repo.id)
384	            if task is None:
385	                print(f"{name:<50s}\t{'waiting for sync':<20s}")
386	                continue
387	
388	            state = task.state
389	            if state in ['uploading', 'downloading']:
390	                tx_task = self.rpc.find_transfer_task(repo.id)
391	                if tx_task.rt_state == "data":
392	                    state += " files"
393	                    percentage = 0 if tx_task.block_done == 0 else tx_task.block_done / tx_task.block_total * 100
394	                    rate = 0 if tx_task.rate == 0 else tx_task.rate / 1024.0
395	                    print(f"{name:<50s}\t{state:<20s}\t{percentage:<.1f}%, {rate:<.1f}KB/s")
396	                elif tx_task.rt_state == "fs":
397	                    state += " files list"
398	                    percentage = 0 if tx_task.fs_objects_done == 0 else tx_task.fs_objects_done / tx_task.fs_objects_total * 100
399	                    print(f"{name:<50s}\t{state:<20s}\t{percentage:<.1f}%")
400	            elif state == 'error':
401	                healthy = False
402	                error = self.rpc.sync_error_id_to_str(task.error)
403	                print(f"{name:<50s}\t{state:<20s}\t{error}")
404	            else:
405	                print(f"{name:<50s}\t{state:<20s}")
406	
407	        return 0 if healthy else 1
408	
409	
410	debug = get_configuration("DEBUG", False)
411	level = logging.INFO
412	fmt = "%(asctime)s - %(levelname)s - %(message)s"
413	if debug:
414	    level = logging.DEBUG
415	    fmt = "%(asctime)s - %(filename)s:%(lineno)d - %(levelname)s - %(message)s"
416	logging.basicConfig(format=fmt, level=level)
417	logger = logging.getLogger()
418	
419	
420	if __name__ == "__main__":
421	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(prog="", description="", epilog="")
422	    parser.add_argument("--healthcheck", action="store_true")
423	    args = parser.parse_args()
424	
425	    if args.healthcheck:
426	        logger.disabled = True
427	
428	    try:
429	        client = Client()
430	    except BadConfiguration as e:
431	        logger.error(f"Bad configuration: {e}")
432	        sys.exit(1)
433	
434	    if args.healthcheck:
435	        if not hasattr(client, "rpc"):
436	            print("seaf-daemon socket not found — daemon is not running")
437	            sys.exit(1)
438	        sys.exit(client.healthcheck())
439	
440	    def _handle_stop(signum, frame):
441	        logger.info("Received signal — stopping seaf-daemon cleanly")
442	        subprocess.run(["seaf-cli", "stop"], timeout=30)
443	        sys.exit(0)
444	
445	    signal.signal(signal.SIGTERM, _handle_stop)
446	    signal.signal(signal.SIGINT, _handle_stop)
447	
448	    client.initialize()
449	    client.configure()
450	    client.synchronize()
451	
452	    settings_url = get_configuration("SEAF_SETTINGS_URL", None)
453	    status_token = get_configuration("SEAF_STATUS_TOKEN", None)
454	    if settings_url:
455	        client._start_status_reporter(settings_url, status_token)
456	
457	    client.watch()","total_lines":457}

payload.message:
Implemented the clearer pause/resume messaging in the repo source on the deployment workspace.

Changed:
- [/worksp/seafile/seafile-server/nas-settings/app.py](/worksp/seafile/seafile-server/nas-settings/app.py): adds schedule-aware status annotations. If a library is paused because the schedule is currently closed, the API now returns a `schedule_note` explaining that a resume can be accepted and then immediately re-paused by the next heartbeat.
- [/worksp/seafile/seafile-server/nas-settings/templates/status.html](/worksp/seafile/seafile-server/nas-settings/templates/status.html): shows a “waiting for heartbeat” note immediately after clicking Pause/Resume, then replaces it with the schedule explanation when applicable.

Verified with:
```text
python3 -m py_compile seafile-server/nas-settings/app.py
git diff --check
```

One caveat: I updated the repo source I can access at `/worksp/seafile`, but I do not have shell access to the Brazil Seafile VPS/running `nas-settings` container from the available tools, so this still needs to be deployed/rebuilt there before the live page changes.

content.text:
Implemented the clearer pause/resume messaging in the repo source on the deployment workspace.

Changed:
- [/worksp/seafile/seafile-server/nas-settings/app.py](/worksp/seafile/seafile-server/nas-settings/app.py): adds schedule-aware status annotations. If a library is paused because the schedule is currently closed, the API now returns a `schedule_note` explaining that a resume can be accepted and then immediately re-paused by the next heartbeat.
- [/worksp/seafile/seafile-server/nas-settings/templates/status.html](/worksp/seafile/seafile-server/nas-settings/templates/status.html): shows a “waiting for heartbeat” note immediately after clicking Pause/Resume, then replaces it with the schedule explanation when applicable.

Verified with:
```text
python3 -m py_compile seafile-server/nas-settings/app.py
git diff --check
```

One caveat: I updated the repo source I can access at `/worksp/seafile`, but I do not have shell access to the Brazil Seafile VPS/running `nas-settings` container from the available tools, so this still needs to be deployed/rebuilt there before the live page changes.

If a library is paused because the schedule is currently closed, the API now returns a `schedule_note` explaining that a resume can be accepted and then immediately re-paused by the next heartbeat.
- [/worksp/seafile/seafile-server/nas-settings/templates/status.html](/worksp/seafile/seafile-server/nas-settings/templates/status.html): shows a “waiting for heartbeat” note immediately after clicking Pause/Resume, then replaces it with the schedule explanation when applicable.

Verified with:

git diff --check

content.text: The public `/nas-settings/status` response is coming from Werkzeug/Python, so the live app is reachable from this host path. It just is not visible in the default `docker ps` list, which means it may be running under another Docker context/user or via compose metadata that is not currently up under the expected name. I’m narrowing that down now.

payload.name: invoke_tool
payload.arguments: {"name":"run_command","args":{"command":"docker context ls 2>/dev/null || true; docker compose ls --all 2>/dev/null || true; find /root /home /opt /var/lib/docker/volumes -maxdepth 5 \\( -iname '*nas-settings*' -o -iname 'status.json' -o -iname 'settings.json' -o -iname 'commands.json' \\) 2>/dev/null | sort | head -200","timeout":60}}

payload.name: invoke_tool
payload.arguments: {"name":"run_command","args":{"command":"find / -maxdepth 6 \\( -path /proc -o -path /sys -o -path /dev -o -path /run \\) -prune -o -type f \\( -name 'status.html' -o -name 'app.py' \\) -print 2>/dev/null | xargs grep -Il 'Sync Controls\\|Restart daemon\\|Stop daemon\\|Live status from the seaf-cli\\|nas-settings' 2>/dev/null | head -200","timeout":60}}

So I’ve made the source change in the repo I can reach at `/worksp/seafile`, but I can’t honestly say it’s applied to the live Seafile VPS process yet.

content.text: wait, it looks like this is NOT running on the seafile VPS. that connection in Codex got severed. i will start a new AI session. give me a long, detailed prompt about everything that's going on, what we're trying to do, where to go in the codebase for the relevant portions, what to do, any background needed, etc. Include every nuance that a fresh developer with no context here would need.

payload.message: wait, it looks like this is NOT running on the seafile VPS. that connection in Codex got severed. i will start a new AI session. give me a long, detailed prompt about everything that's going on, what we're trying to do, where to go in the codebase for the relevant portions, what to do, any background needed, etc. Include every nuance that a fresh developer with no context here would need.

Here’s a prompt you can paste into a fresh Codex session: ```text We are working on a Seafile deployment for seafile.designflow.app. Important correction: the current Codex/devops connection is NOT on the Seafile VPS. It appears to be connected to a Hetzner/Coolify host named `hetz` with public IP `178.156.180.212`, while `seafile.designflow.app` resolves to `172.233.14.233`. So any live deploy/restart must happen from the real Seafile VPS, not the Hetzner box. Goal: Improve the NAS sync control UI so Pause/Resume is more verbose and less misleading, especially when a resume command is accepted but the library remains paused because an active schedule immediately turns sync back off. Background: There is a Seafile server at: - `https://seafile.designflow.app` There is a NAS called `edgesynology1` in NYC running a Docker container named `seaf-cli`. That container syncs NAS folders/libraries into Seafile using `seaf-cli`. Relevant libraries seen in the UI: - Character Licensed - UUID: `177cf9de-3066-482e-956a-7ae8d8786c6d` - Generic Decor - UUID: `1b116ab7-d66b-4411-a691-21f34eadb731` - Style Guides / Styleguides - UUID: `b6e1d4c9-434e-4d8a-bde2-7f19be9c0838` Observed issue: The admin page `/nas-settings/status` or similar “Sync Controls” page showed: - “Paused” - “Last seen 7s ago” - Button still says “Resume sync” - Success alert says `✓ resume: resumed 1 repo(s)` This looked contradictory. We investigated `edgesynology1` and confirmed the actual `seaf-cli` state: ```text Generic Decor auto sync disabled Character Licensed auto sync disabled Styleguides uploading / committing ``` The logs on `edgesynology1` showed the resume command was received: ```text 2026-06-16 14:09:38 UTC Executing queued command for 177cf9de-3066-482e-956a-7ae8d8786c6d: resume 2026-06-16 14:09:39 UTC Executing queued command for 1b116ab7-d66b-4411-a691-21f34eadb731: resume ``` But those libraries remained paused because schedule enforcement was active. The public settings endpoint returned schedules like: ```json { "seaf-cli-char-licensed": { "ingest_days": 730, "schedule": { "enabled": true, "timezone": "America/New_York", "windows": { "weekdays": { "days": [0,1,2,3,4], "enabled": true, "start": "19:00", "end": "08:00" }, "weekends": { "days": [5,6], "enabled": true, "start": "00:00", "end": "23:59" } } }, "uuid": "177cf9de-3066-482e-956a-7ae8d8786c6d" } } ``` Meaning: on weekdays, sync is allowed only from 7:00 PM to 8:00 AM America/New_York. If you click Resume during business hours, the command can technically succeed, but the next heartbeat/schedule pass sets `auto-sync` back to false. The UI should explain this instead of implying the resume failed mysteriously. Relevant codebase: Look for a repo like: ```bash /worksp/seafile /home/ai/seafile-repo /opt/seafile ``` The relevant files are expected to be: ```text seafile-server/nas-settings/app.py seafile-server/nas-settings/templates/status.html synology-seaf-cli/entrypoint.py seafile-server/nas-settings.yml ``` What these do: 1. `seafile-server/nas-settings/app.py` - Flask app behind `/nas-settings/`. - Has endpoints like: - `/api/status`: receives heartbeat/status reports from `seaf-cli`. - `/api/status-data`: returns status to the browser UI. - `/api/command`: queues commands like pause/resume. - `/api/settings`: returns ingest settings and schedules for the `seaf-cli` container. - Persists state in `/data/settings.json`, `/data/status.json`, and `/data/commands.json`. 2. `seafile-server/nas-settings/templates/status.html` - Browser UI for sync status/controls. - Renders cards for libraries. - Sends Pause/Resume commands to `/api/command`. - Polls `/api/status-data`. 3. `synology-seaf-cli/entrypoint.py` - Runs in the NAS `seaf-cli` container. - Starts `seaf-cli`, reports status to `/nas-settings/api/status`, receives queued commands in the response. - In the current/live version, pause/resume is implemented per repo by setting the repo property `auto-sync` to `"false"` or `"true"`. - It can also apply schedule rules from settings, which is why resume may be undone by the schedule. Important live diagnostic commands from `edgesynology1`: If NAS MCP or SSH is available, these were useful: ```bash /var/packages/ContainerManager/target/usr/bin/docker ps -a --format 'table {{.Names}}\t{{.Image}}\t{{.Status}}' /var/packages/ContainerManager/target/usr/bin/docker logs --tail 200 seaf-cli /var/packages/ContainerManager/target/usr/bin/docker exec seaf-cli sh -lc 'seaf-cli status; seaf-cli list' /var/packages/ContainerManager/target/usr/bin/docker exec seaf-cli python3 -c "import json,urllib.request; print(json.dumps(json.load(urllib.request.urlopen('https://seafile.designflow.app/nas-settings/api/settings',timeout=10)), indent=2))" ``` Expected result currently: `Styleguides` may be uploading, while Character Licensed and/or Generic Decor may be paused depending on schedule settings. What to implement: Make pause/resume status more verbose. Minimum desired behavior: - When the user clicks Pause or Resume, immediately show something like: - `Resume requested. Waiting for the next seaf-cli heartbeat to confirm the actual state.` - If the next status says the library is still paused and its schedule is currently closed, show a warning/note like: - `Schedule is currently outside the sync window (weekdays 19:00-08:00 America/New_York). Resume was accepted, but the next heartbeat paused sync again. It will resume automatically when the schedule opens, or disable/change the schedule to sync now.` - The Resume button may remain clickable if paused, but the explanation should make it clear this is schedule-controlled. Suggested implementation approach: In `app.py`: - Import `ZoneInfo`: ```python from zoneinfo import ZoneInfo ``` - Add helper functions to: - Find the settings entry for a status row by container id or library UUID. - Evaluate whether the schedule currently allows sync. - Summarize the schedule in human text. Pseudo-code: ```python def _library_settings_for_status(settings: dict, cid: str, entry: dict) -> dict: if cid in settings: return settings[cid] library_uuid = entry.get("library_uuid") if library_uuid: for value in settings.values(): if isinstance(value, dict) and value.get("uuid") == library_uuid: return value return {} ``` Schedule evaluator must support overnight windows like `19:00` to `08:00`: ```python def _schedule_allows_sync(schedule: dict | None, now: datetime.datetime | None = None) -> bool: if not schedule or not schedule.get("enabled"): return True try: tz = ZoneInfo(schedule.get("timezone") or "UTC") except Exception: tz = datetime.timezone.utc now = now.astimezone(tz) if now else datetime.datetime.now(tz) def minutes(value: str, fallback: str) -> int: try: hour, minute = value.split(":", 1) return int(hour) * 60 + int(minute) except Exception: hour, minute = fallback.split(":", 1) return int(hour) * 60 + int(minute) current = now.hour * 60 + now.minute today = now.weekday() yesterday = (today - 1) % 7 def window_allows(window: dict) -> bool: if window.get("enabled") is False: return False days = set(window.get("days") or []) if not days: return False start = minutes(window.get("start", "00:00"), "00:00") end = minutes(window.get("end", "23:59"), "23:59") if start == end: return today in days if start < end: return today in days and start <= current < end # Overnight window, e.g. 19:00 -> 08:00. return (today in days and current >= start) or (yesterday in days and current < end) windows = schedule.get("windows") if isinstance(windows, dict): return any(window_allows(window) for window in windows.values() if isinstance(window, dict)) return window_allows({ "enabled": True, "days": schedule.get("days") or [], "start": schedule.get("start", "00:00"), "end": schedule.get("end", "23:59"), }) ``` Then in `/api/status-data`: - Load settings. - For each status entry, attach: - `schedule` - `schedule_allows_sync` - `schedule_note` Pseudo-code: ```python settings = load_settings() ... lib_settings = _library_settings_for_status(settings, cid, entry) schedule = lib_settings.get("schedule") if isinstance(lib_settings, dict) else None schedule_allows_sync = _schedule_allows_sync(schedule) schedule_note = None if schedule and schedule.get("enabled") and not schedule_allows_sync: schedule_note = _schedule_summary(schedule) if entry.get("paused"): schedule_note += " Resume can be accepted, then the next heartbeat will pause it again until the schedule opens." result[cid] = { **entry, "stale": stale, "seconds_ago": seconds_ago, "schedule": schedule, "schedule_allows_sync": schedule_allows_sync, "schedule_note": schedule_note, } ``` Also make sure `/api/settings` returns schedule data, not just `ingest_days` and `uuid`. In an older repo copy, this endpoint dropped the schedule. It should include the whole entry: ```python result[lid] = { **entry, "ingest_days": entry.get("ingest_days", DEFAULT_INGEST_DAYS), "uuid": lib["uuid"], } ``` In `status.html`: - Add CSS for command notes, e.g. blue info and amber warning. - Track pending commands in JS: ```javascript const pendingCommands = {}; ``` - In `sendCommand`, before/after POST: - Disable button. - Store pending message. - Call refresh after sending. Example: ```javascript async function sendCommand(containerId, command, btn) { btn.disabled = true; const verb = command === "resume" ? Try again."); } finally { btn.disabled = false; refresh(); } } ``` - Add a function to render notes safely: ```javascript function escapeHtml(value) { return String(value).replace(/[&<>"']/g, ch => ({ "&": "&", "<": "<", ">": ">", '"': """, "'": "'", })[ch]); } function commandNote(lib, data) { const pending = pendingCommands[lib.id]; if (data && !data.stale && data.schedule_note) { pendingCommands[lib.id] = null; return `

${label}

Here’s a prompt you can paste into a fresh Codex session: ```text We are working on a Seafile deployment for seafile.designflow.app. Important correction: the current Codex/devops connection is NOT on the Seafile VPS. It appears to be connected to a Hetzner/Coolify host named `hetz` with public IP `178.156.180.212`, while `seafile.designflow.app` resolves to `172.233.14.233`. So any live deploy/restart must happen from the real Seafile VPS, not the Hetzner box. Goal: Improve the NAS sync control UI so Pause/Resume is more verbose and less misleading, especially when a resume command is accepted but the library remains paused because an active schedule immediately turns sync back off. Background: There is a Seafile server at: - `https://seafile.designflow.app` There is a NAS called `edgesynology1` in NYC running a Docker container named `seaf-cli`. That container syncs NAS folders/libraries into Seafile using `seaf-cli`. Relevant libraries seen in the UI: - Character Licensed - UUID: `177cf9de-3066-482e-956a-7ae8d8786c6d` - Generic Decor - UUID: `1b116ab7-d66b-4411-a691-21f34eadb731` - Style Guides / Styleguides - UUID: `b6e1d4c9-434e-4d8a-bde2-7f19be9c0838` Observed issue: The admin page `/nas-settings/status` or similar “Sync Controls” page showed: - “Paused” - “Last seen 7s ago” - Button still says “Resume sync” - Success alert says `✓ resume: resumed 1 repo(s)` This looked contradictory. We investigated `edgesynology1` and confirmed the actual `seaf-cli` state: ```text Generic Decor auto sync disabled Character Licensed auto sync disabled Styleguides uploading / committing ``` The logs on `edgesynology1` showed the resume command was received: ```text 2026-06-16 14:09:38 UTC Executing queued command for 177cf9de-3066-482e-956a-7ae8d8786c6d: resume 2026-06-16 14:09:39 UTC Executing queued command for 1b116ab7-d66b-4411-a691-21f34eadb731: resume ``` But those libraries remained paused because schedule enforcement was active. The public settings endpoint returned schedules like: ```json { "seaf-cli-char-licensed": { "ingest_days": 730, "schedule": { "enabled": true, "timezone": "America/New_York", "windows": { "weekdays": { "days": [0,1,2,3,4], "enabled": true, "start": "19:00", "end": "08:00" }, "weekends": { "days": [5,6], "enabled": true, "start": "00:00", "end": "23:59" } } }, "uuid": "177cf9de-3066-482e-956a-7ae8d8786c6d" } } ``` Meaning: on weekdays, sync is allowed only from 7:00 PM to 8:00 AM America/New_York. If you click Resume during business hours, the command can technically succeed, but the next heartbeat/schedule pass sets `auto-sync` back to false. The UI should explain this instead of implying the resume failed mysteriously. Relevant codebase: Look for a repo like: ```bash /worksp/seafile /home/ai/seafile-repo /opt/seafile ``` The relevant files are expected to be: ```text seafile-server/nas-settings/app.py seafile-server/nas-settings/templates/status.html synology-seaf-cli/entrypoint.py seafile-server/nas-settings.yml ``` What these do: 1. `seafile-server/nas-settings/app.py` - Flask app behind `/nas-settings/`. - Has endpoints like: - `/api/status`: receives heartbeat/status reports from `seaf-cli`. - `/api/status-data`: returns status to the browser UI. - `/api/command`: queues commands like pause/resume. - `/api/settings`: returns ingest settings and schedules for the `seaf-cli` container. - Persists state in `/data/settings.json`, `/data/status.json`, and `/data/commands.json`. 2. `seafile-server/nas-settings/templates/status.html` - Browser UI for sync status/controls. - Renders cards for libraries. - Sends Pause/Resume commands to `/api/command`. - Polls `/api/status-data`. 3. `synology-seaf-cli/entrypoint.py` - Runs in the NAS `seaf-cli` container. - Starts `seaf-cli`, reports status to `/nas-settings/api/status`, receives queued commands in the response. - In the current/live version, pause/resume is implemented per repo by setting the repo property `auto-sync` to `"false"` or `"true"`. - It can also apply schedule rules from settings, which is why resume may be undone by the schedule. Important live diagnostic commands from `edgesynology1`: If NAS MCP or SSH is available, these were useful: ```bash /var/packages/ContainerManager/target/usr/bin/docker ps -a --format 'table {{.Names}}\t{{.Image}}\t{{.Status}}' /var/packages/ContainerManager/target/usr/bin/docker logs --tail 200 seaf-cli /var/packages/ContainerManager/target/usr/bin/docker exec seaf-cli sh -lc 'seaf-cli status; seaf-cli list' /var/packages/ContainerManager/target/usr/bin/docker exec seaf-cli python3 -c "import json,urllib.request; print(json.dumps(json.load(urllib.request.urlopen('https://seafile.designflow.app/nas-settings/api/settings',timeout=10)), indent=2))" ``` Expected result currently: `Styleguides` may be uploading, while Character Licensed and/or Generic Decor may be paused depending on schedule settings. What to implement: Make pause/resume status more verbose. Minimum desired behavior: - When the user clicks Pause or Resume, immediately show something like: - `Resume requested. Waiting for the next seaf-cli heartbeat to confirm the actual state.` - If the next status says the library is still paused and its schedule is currently closed, show a warning/note like: - `Schedule is currently outside the sync window (weekdays 19:00-08:00 America/New_York). Resume was accepted, but the next heartbeat paused sync again. It will resume automatically when the schedule opens, or disable/change the schedule to sync now.` - The Resume button may remain clickable if paused, but the explanation should make it clear this is schedule-controlled. Suggested implementation approach: In `app.py`: - Import `ZoneInfo`: ```python from zoneinfo import ZoneInfo ``` - Add helper functions to: - Find the settings entry for a status row by container id or library UUID. - Evaluate whether the schedule currently allows sync. - Summarize the schedule in human text. Pseudo-code: ```python def _library_settings_for_status(settings: dict, cid: str, entry: dict) -> dict: if cid in settings: return settings[cid] library_uuid = entry.get("library_uuid") if library_uuid: for value in settings.values(): if isinstance(value, dict) and value.get("uuid") == library_uuid: return value return {} ``` Schedule evaluator must support overnight windows like `19:00` to `08:00`: ```python def _schedule_allows_sync(schedule: dict | None, now: datetime.datetime | None = None) -> bool: if not schedule or not schedule.get("enabled"): return True try: tz = ZoneInfo(schedule.get("timezone") or "UTC") except Exception: tz = datetime.timezone.utc now = now.astimezone(tz) if now else datetime.datetime.now(tz) def minutes(value: str, fallback: str) -> int: try: hour, minute = value.split(":", 1) return int(hour) * 60 + int(minute) except Exception: hour, minute = fallback.split(":", 1) return int(hour) * 60 + int(minute) current = now.hour * 60 + now.minute today = now.weekday() yesterday = (today - 1) % 7 def window_allows(window: dict) -> bool: if window.get("enabled") is False: return False days = set(window.get("days") or []) if not days: return False start = minutes(window.get("start", "00:00"), "00:00") end = minutes(window.get("end", "23:59"), "23:59") if start == end: return today in days if start < end: return today in days and start <= current < end # Overnight window, e.g. 19:00 -> 08:00. return (today in days and current >= start) or (yesterday in days and current < end) windows = schedule.get("windows") if isinstance(windows, dict): return any(window_allows(window) for window in windows.values() if isinstance(window, dict)) return window_allows({ "enabled": True, "days": schedule.get("days") or [], "start": schedule.get("start", "00:00"), "end": schedule.get("end", "23:59"), }) ``` Then in `/api/status-data`: - Load settings. - For each status entry, attach: - `schedule` - `schedule_allows_sync` - `schedule_note` Pseudo-code: ```python settings = load_settings() ... lib_settings = _library_settings_for_status(settings, cid, entry) schedule = lib_settings.get("schedule") if isinstance(lib_settings, dict) else None schedule_allows_sync = _schedule_allows_sync(schedule) schedule_note = None if schedule and schedule.get("enabled") and not schedule_allows_sync: schedule_note = _schedule_summary(schedule) if entry.get("paused"): schedule_note += " Resume can be accepted, then the next heartbeat will pause it again until the schedule opens." result[cid] = { **entry, "stale": stale, "seconds_ago": seconds_ago, "schedule": schedule, "schedule_allows_sync": schedule_allows_sync, "schedule_note": schedule_note, } ``` Also make sure `/api/settings` returns schedule data, not just `ingest_days` and `uuid`. In an older repo copy, this endpoint dropped the schedule. It should include the whole entry: ```python result[lid] = { **entry, "ingest_days": entry.get("ingest_days", DEFAULT_INGEST_DAYS), "uuid": lib["uuid"], } ``` In `status.html`: - Add CSS for command notes, e.g. blue info and amber warning. - Track pending commands in JS: ```javascript const pendingCommands = {}; ``` - In `sendCommand`, before/after POST: - Disable button. - Store pending message. - Call refresh after sending. Example: ```javascript async function sendCommand(containerId, command, btn) { btn.disabled = true; const verb = command === "resume" ? "Resume" : "Pause"; pendingCommands[containerId] = `${verb} requested. Waiting for the next seaf-cli heartbeat to confirm the actual state.`; try { const resp = await fetch("{{ url_for('api_command') }}", { method: "POST", headers: {"Content-Type": "application/json"}, body: JSON.stringify({container_id: containerId, command}), }); if (!resp.ok) throw new Error("server error"); } catch (e) { pendingCommands[containerId] = null; alert("Failed to send command. Try again."); } finally { btn.disabled = false; refresh(); } } ``` - Add a function to render notes safely: ```javascript function escapeHtml(value) { return String(value).replace(/[&<>"']/g, ch => ({ "&": "&", "<": "<", ">": ">", '"': """, "'": "'", })[ch]); } function commandNote(lib, data) { const pending = pendingCommands[lib.id]; if (data && !data.stale && data.schedule_note) { pendingCommands[lib.id] = null; return `
${escapeHtml(data.schedule_note)}
`; } if (pending) { return `
${escapeHtml(pending)}
`; } return ""; } ``` - In `renderCard`, insert the note just below the status badge and above progress/metagrid: ```javascript const note = commandNote(lib, data); bodyEl.innerHTML = `
${label}
${note} ${progressBar} ... `; ``` Caveat: The running/live UI seen in screenshot had “Sync Controls”, “Restart daemon”, and “Stop daemon”, which may be newer than the repo copy on the Hetz host. Verification: On the Seafile VPS: ```bash cd /path/to/seafile-repo python3 -m py_compile seafile-server/nas-settings/app.py git diff --check ``` If Docker compose is used for nas-settings: ```bash cd /path/to/seafile-repo/seafile-server docker compose -f nas-settings.yml up -d --build nas-settings ``` Then check: ```bash curl -I https://seafile.designflow.app/nas-settings/status curl -s https://seafile.designflow.app/nas-settings/api/settings | python3 -m json.tool ``` After deployment: - Open `https://seafile.designflow.app/nas-settings/status`. - Click Resume on a library whose schedule is currently closed. - Expected: immediate “waiting for heartbeat” note. - After next heartbeat/status refresh: a warning explaining that the schedule is currently outside sync hours and will pause again until the window opens. Related but separate issues discovered: - The `edgesynology1` Synology Drive ShareSync daemon had errors like `Daemon is not alive`, but that is Synology ShareSync and is separate from the Seafile `seaf-cli` container. The active Seafile upload path is `seaf-cli`, not Synology Drive ShareSync. - `seaf-cli pause` / `seaf-cli resume` are not valid commands in the base image. The wrapper implements pause/resume using Seafile RPC by setting each repo’s `auto-sync` property. - The dashboard may show “resumed 1 repo(s)” because the RPC property set succeeded, but schedule enforcement later flips it off again. Security note: Do not paste or expose secrets from container env vars. Earlier inspection showed `SEAF_PASSWORD` and `SEAF_STATUS_TOKEN` in the NAS container environment.
